content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660        322-5660           322-3901          322-6441        322-6444

May 5, 1980


John D. Jorgenson
City Attorney
1100 Alma Street
Menlo Park, CA 94025

Dear Mr. Jorgenson:

     This is in response to your letter of April 29, 1980, in which you ask whether the Political Reform Act prohibits participation in a decision by a government official if the decision will have a material financial effect on a source of income to the official's spouse.

     Government Code Section 82030 defines the term "income" as used in the Political Reform Act to mean "a payment received, including but not limited to any salary, wage, advance, . . . and including any community property interest in income of a spouse. . . ." Therefore, when Government Code Section 87103(c) refers to "any source of income. . . aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value. . ." it by definition includes sources of community property income. Since each spouse's share of community property income is a one-half share, an official is not required to disqualify unless his or her spouse receives $500 or more in community property income from the source.

     If you have any further questions concerning this matter, please do not hesitate to call me.

                    Sincerely,

                    Sarah T. Cameron
                    Deputy Chief for
                    Conflicts of Interest
                    Legal Division

STC:nrw

DOUGLAS W. DUPEN
MAYOR

BILLY RAY WHITE
MAYOR PRO TEM

GERRY B. ANDEEN
COUNCILMEMBER

ROBERT J. STEPHENS
COUNCILMEMBER

JAMES L. BLOCH
COUNCILMEMBER

OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY

CITY OF
MENLO
PARK

1100 ALMA STREET / MENLO PARK, CALIFORNIA 94025 / TELEPHONE (415) 323-7711

April 29, 1980

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 607
Sacramento, California 95804

Attn:   Conflict of Interest
        Legal Division

Dear Sir/Madam:

     I am the City Attorney of the City of Menlo Park.  A member of
our City Council is married to a person who is employed by Stanford
University.  Stanford University owns property in the City of Menlo
Park and is proposing to enter into a long-term ground lease with a
developer who contemplates the construction on the subject property
of a motel.  This would require rezoning and possibly other action
by the City Council.

     We also have a member of the City Council who is employed by
Stanford University.

     It seems to me that Government Code §87100 precludes participa-
tion by the councilman who is employed by Stanford University on the
grounds that he has a financial interest in the decision because the
decision will have a material financial effect on a business entity in
which the public official is an employee.  There however seems to be
no statutory answer to the question as to whether or not such employment
by the spouse of the public official would disqualify the public
official.  It seems that it would necessarily disqualify the public
official in view of the community property nature of the wages that
would be earned.  My preliminary determination in this matter has been
that such employment by the spouse of the public official would dis-
qualify the public official from taking action.

     We have five people on the City Council, and if my tentative
position holds, two will be disqualified, which means that all three
will have to unanimously support any rezoning action; so, the decision
in this regard is fairly important.  I would appreciate it if you
could give me your thoughts on this subject and advise me whether or
not an application for a formal ruling from the F.P.P.C. would be
appropriate.

                                        Yours very truly,

                                        John D. Jorgenson
                                        City Attorney

JDJ/fjb

sections.question: Whether Government Code §87100 precludes participation by a City Council member who is employed by Stanford University in decisions affecting Stanford University property, and whether employment of a Council member's spouse by Stanford University would similarly disqualify the Council member due to community property considerations.
sections.question_synthetic: The City Attorney asks whether Government Code §87100 disqualifies a City Council member employed by Stanford University from voting on a rezoning matter affecting Stanford University property, and whether the spouse's employment by Stanford University would similarly disqualify the official.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response letter. Document quality is poor with significant scanning artifacts and illegible sections. The letter is dated April 29, 1980, from John D. [last name illegible] at Menlo Park City Attorney's office to FPPC. The document appears to be the original incoming request rather than the FPPC's advice response. No formal conclusion or advice section is present.

embedding.summary: City Attorney of Menlo Park requests FPPC guidance on conflict of interest disqualification rules under Government Code §87100 for two City Council members with employment connections to Stanford University regarding a proposed motel development project.
